era_context: The late 10th century CE: The Byzantine Empire under Nikephoros II Phokas was at the height of its military power; the Fatimid Caliphate was consolidating control in North Africa and preparing to expand into Egypt; the Song Dynasty was consolidating control over China following the Five Dynasties period; the Ottonian Holy Roman Empire was expanding in Central Europe; Scandinavian Christianization was accelerating. This was an era of major political and military transformations across Eurasia.
geographic_coverage_gaps: Pre-Columbian Americas (no Old World written records)

events.title: Byzantine-Fatimid Conflict in the Levant
events.region: Syria / Levant / Byzantine Empire / Fatimid Caliphate
events.description: The Byzantine Empire under Nikephoros II Phokas and the Fatimid Caliphate were in conflict over control of the Levant. Byzantine forces, under Nikephoros II's aggressive military leadership, were pushing into northern Syria and the Levantine coast, while the Fatimids were consolidating their power in Egypt and the southern Levant. This conflict would continue throughout the late 10th century, with the Byzantines gaining the upper hand in the 970s.
key_figures: Emir of Aleppo (Hamdanid ruler, caught between powers)
events.certainty_note: The broader Byzantine-Fatimid conflict in the Levant is well-attested; specific events in 968 CE are less precisely dated. The chronology of this conflict spans multiple years with some uncertainty in year-by-year attribution.

events.title: Song Dynasty Consolidation: Conquest of Nanzhao
events.description: The Song Dynasty, under Emperor Taizu, conquered the Nanzhao kingdom in southwestern China (modern Yunnan). This was a significant step in Song reunification, bringing the southwestern territories under Song control. The conquest was achieved through military campaigns and diplomatic pressure, and it extended Song authority to the borders of Southeast Asia.

sources.name: Zizhi Tongjian (Comprehensive Mirror for Aid in Government)
Song Shi (History of the Song Dynasty)

events.certainty_note: The conquest of Nanzhao is well-documented in Chinese historical records. The date 968 CE is the conventional assignment in modern scholarship.

events.title: Ottonian Expansion: Otto I's Campaigns in Poland
events.region: Central Europe / Poland / Holy Roman Empire
events.description: Otto I, Holy Roman Emperor, conducted campaigns in Poland to establish Ottonian suzerainty and promote Christianization. These campaigns were part of a broader strategy to extend German and Christian influence eastward. Otto I's military and diplomatic campaigns laid the groundwork for the later expansion of German influence in Eastern Europe and the Christianization of Poland.
events.certainty_note: Otto I's broader campaigns in Eastern Europe are well-attested; specific events in 968 CE are less precisely dated. The chronology of Otto I's Eastern European campaigns spans multiple years with some uncertainty in year-by-year attribution.
cross_references: 970-003 (Mieszko I's baptism, related to Ottonian influence)

events.title: Fatimid Consolidation in North Africa
events.description: The Fatimid Caliphate under Al-Muizz li-Din Allah consolidated its power in North Africa while preparing for the conquest of Egypt. The Fatimids were strengthening their administrative institutions and military capabilities in preparation for the major expansion into Egypt that would occur in 969 CE.
events.certainty_note: Fatimid consolidation in North Africa is well-documented in the broader context of the late 10th century; specific events in 968 CE are less precisely dated. This entry reflects the general trajectory of Fatimid power during this period.

disconfirming_evidence: The dating of specific Byzantine-Fatimid conflicts in 968 CE is uncertain; the broader conflict is confirmed, but year-by-year attribution is problematic. The dating of the Song Dynasty conquest of Nanzhao is sometimes placed in 967 CE or 969 CE in some sources, creating ±1 year uncertainty. The specific events attributed to Otto I's campaigns in 968 CE are less well-documented than the broader campaign trajectory.
historiographic_note: The historical record for 968 CE is dominated by Byzantine, Western European, and Chinese sources. Arab and Islamic sources are well-preserved but often compiled centuries later. Byzantine sources emphasize military campaigns; Chinese sources emphasize administrative consolidation and dynastic legitimacy. The dating of specific events in 968 CE is less precise than for later years, reflecting the general pattern of increasing documentary precision as one approaches the present. Sub-Saharan African, American, and Central Asian histories remain largely absent from contemporary written records.

graph_edges.note: Byzantine-Fatimid conflict in 968 led to Nikephoros II's campaigns in 969
Nanzhao conquest is part of broader Song reunification
Fatimid consolidation in North Africa led to conquest of Egypt in 969
